nl_statement: If f is convex on s and has gradient f'(x) at every x ∈ s, then ⟨f'(x) - f'(y), x - y⟩ ≥ 0 for all x, y ∈ s.
lean_signature: theorem convex_monotone_gradient_prime_helper_depth1_idx2 {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E] {s : Set E} {f : E → ℝ} {f' : E → E} (hfun : ConvexOn ℝ s f) (h : ∀ x ∈ s, HasGradientAt f (f' x) x) : ∀ x ∈ s, ∀ y ∈ s, inner ℝ (f' x - f' y) (x - y) ≥ (0 : ℝ)
assumptions: f has gradient f' on s
